content: I'm thinking about inviting my parents over for dinner this weekend. They've been wanting to see my new apartment since I moved in last month.
That sounds like a great idea! What's your new place like? I'd love to hear about the sustainable design features you've incorporated.
It's a converted warehouse loft in the Strip District. I've got reclaimed wood floors and a living wall in the living room. The building has solar panels too.
That's amazing! Your parents must be so proud of your work. What are you thinking of cooking for dinner?
I was thinking of making my mom's favorite - chicken piccata. Though I need to remember my roommate Alex is vegan now, so I'll need a separate dish for her.
Good thinking about Alex. Maybe a stuffed portobello mushroom or vegan lasagna would work? What's your mom like to cook with?
She's actually a retired chef. We used to bake together when I was a kid. I still remember her teaching me how to make perfect pie crust when I was 12.
That's such a special memory. Do you still bake together when you visit? Maybe you could make a dessert together this weekend.
We usually do. Last time we made an apple galette with apples from my dad's garden. Speaking of dad, I should probably check if he's okay with the drive from their place in Monroeville.
That's thoughtful of you. How long is the drive for them? Maybe you could pick them up if it's a hassle.
About 30 minutes without traffic. They're pretty independent though. My dad still works part-time at the hardware store he's owned for 25 years.
That's wonderful that they're still so active. Sounds like you have a close family. I'm sure they'll love seeing your new sustainable home.
